questions: I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see a man in the parking lot of a hospital leaning against his car, looking at the ground. He's been there a while. Someone asks me what I observe.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I'm in a waiting room and there's a man in the corner who has been sitting very still for a long time, staring at the floor. Someone asks me what I notice.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I'm at a funeral watching the deceased's adult children stand at the front of the room, receiving people. Someone asks me what I observe.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see a woman in the park sitting on a bench, not looking at anything, with a card in her hands. Someone asks me what I notice about her.
I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I pass an older man on the sidewalk who is walking slowly with his head down. Someone asks what I notice about him.
I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see a woman at a bus stop sitting very still, hands in her lap, not checking her phone. Someone asks what I notice.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I'm in a diner and I notice a man sitting alone at a table, not eating, just looking out the window. Someone asks what I observe about him.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see a young man standing outside a building, not moving, with his hands in his pockets. He looks like he's been there for a while. What do I notice?

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see a woman sitting in a car in a parking lot, not driving, just sitting. She's been there for a while. Someone asks what I observe.

I stay detached from other people's circumstances — I observe without absorbing. I see an older woman at a pharmacy counter, looking at a prescription bag, standing very still for a moment. Someone asks what I notice.
